name: 001_interactive_n_declines_overwrite.sh
stderr: destination was overwritten despite negative interactive response
exercises: -i prompts before overwrite and a negative response must prevent the overwrite, even when stdin is a pipe; impl auto-confirms when stdin is not a tty
expected: destination remains with its original contents after answering n to the interactive overwrite prompt

name: 002_interactive_eof_declines_overwrite.sh
stderr: destination was overwritten despite EOF at interactive prompt
exercises: -i prompts before overwrite; EOF provides no affirmative response, but impl treats non-tty stdin as yes
expected: destination remains unchanged when interactive overwrite receives EOF

name: 003_final_i_after_f_controls_prompt.sh
stderr: final -i did not prevent overwrite after negative response
exercises: when -f and -i are both specified, final -i takes effect and prompts before overwrite; impl still auto-confirms on non-tty stdin
expected: destination remains unchanged after -f -i and an n response
manpage_quote:
-i, --interactive
              prompt before overwrite

       -n, --no-clobber
              do not overwrite an existing file

       If you specify more than one of -i, -f, -n, only the final one takes
       effect.

name: 004_strip_trailing_slashes_moves_symlink_itself.sh
exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes removes trailing slashes from SOURCE before moving, including a symlink source; impl deliberately keeps slash on symlink operands
expected: destination is a symbolic link moved from the slash-stripped source operand
manpage_quote:
--strip-trailing-slashes
              remove any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument

name: 005_strip_trailing_slashes_does_not_apply_to_dest.sh
stderr: destination trailing slash was stripped even though only SOURCE slashes are documented
exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes applies to SOURCE arguments only; impl maps the stripping function over all operands including DEST
expected: mv fails rather than silently creating the destination with its trailing slash removed
manpage_quote:
--strip-trailing-slashes
              remove any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument

name: 006_backup_existing_detects_high_numbered_backup.sh
stderr: existing numbered backup did not select next numbered backup
exercises: --backup=existing uses numbered backups if numbered backups already exist; impl only detects a preexisting .~1~ backup
expected: old destination is backed up as the next numbered backup, dst.~6~
manpage_quote:
existing, nil
              numbered if numbered backups exist, simple otherwise

name: 007_backup_suffix_override_simple_backup.sh
exercises: --suffix overrides the usual backup suffix for simple backups
expected: existing destination is backed up using the .bak suffix
manpage_quote:
The backup suffix is '~', unless set with --suffix or
       SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX.

name: 008_backup_numbered_creates_next_number.sh
expected: existing destination is backed up as dst.~2~ when dst.~1~ already exists

name: 009_update_older_skips_newer_destination.sh
exercises: --update with no argument defaults to older and replaces only when destination is older than source
manpage_quote:
'older' is the default operation when --update is specified, and
       results in files being replaced if they're older than the corresponding
       source file.

name: 010_update_all_default_replaces_even_newer_destination.sh
exercises: without --update, update mode all replaces existing destination files regardless of timestamps
expected: destination is replaced by the source contents
manpage_quote:
'all' is the default operation when an --update option is not
       specified, and results in all existing files in the destination being
       replaced.

name: 011_update_none_fail_existing_destination_errors.sh
exercises: --update=none-fail diagnoses skipped existing destinations and induces failure
expected: mv exits nonzero when an existing destination is skipped under --update=none-fail
manpage_quote:
'none-fail' also ensures no files are replaced in the
       destination, but any skipped files are diagnosed and induce a failure.

name: 012_invalid_update_value_errors.sh
exercises: --update accepts only the documented UPDATE values
expected: mv exits nonzero for an invalid --update value

name: 013_target_directory_must_be_directory.sh
expected: mv exits nonzero when -t names a regular file instead of a directory
manpage_quote:
-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY
              move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY

name: 014_no_target_directory_treats_dest_as_file.sh
exercises: -T treats DEST as a normal file rather than as a target directory
expected: mv exits nonzero when asked with -T to replace an existing directory with a regular file
manpage_quote:
-T, --no-target-directory
              treat DEST as a normal file
